questions.text: Sodium Benzoate on reacting with soda lime forms:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: False. While benzoic acid is related to sodium benzoate, the reaction with soda lime does not produce benzoic acid. Instead, the decarboxylation process releases carbon dioxide and yields benzene.
Correct! The reaction of sodium benzoate with soda lime results in decarboxylation, leading to the formation of benzene and sodium carbonate.
False. Toluene is not produced when sodium benzoate reacts with soda lime. The correct product from this reaction is benzene, which has a different structure than toluene.
False. Benzaldehyde is not the product of this reaction. The decarboxylation of sodium benzoate yields benzene instead.

questions.explanation: Sodium benzoate undergoes decarboxylation when heated with soda lime (NaOH + CaO). The -COONa group is removed as CO2, leaving behind toluene (C6H5CH3).This is a classic example of alkali-induced decarboxylation of a carboxylate salt.

questions.text: Which of the following is the strongest acid?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Propanoic acid (propionic acid) is weaker than trichloroethanoic acid.
Fluoroethanoic acid (fluoroacetic acid) is weaker than trichloroethanoic acid.

Among the given options, trichloroethanoic acid is the strongest acid.The strength of an acid is determined by its ability to donate protons (H+ ions) in an aqueous solution. Acids with more electronegative atoms or more electron-withdrawing groups tend to be stronger acids because they stabilize the negative charge on the conjugate base, making it easier to release a proton.Trichloroethanoic acid (also known as trichloroacetic acid) has three chlorine atoms attached to the acetic acid (ethanoic acid) backbone. The chlorine atoms are highly electronegative, which stabilizes the negative charge on the conjugate base (trichloroacetate ion) and facilitates the release of a proton, making it a strong acid.The other options are weaker acids compared to trichloroethanoic acid:Propanoic acid (propionic acid) is weaker than trichloroethanoic acid.Fluoroethanoic acid (fluoroacetic acid) is weaker than trichloroethanoic acid.Nitroethanoic acid (nitroacetic acid) is weaker than trichloroethanoic acid.So, trichloroethanoic acid is the strongest acid among the given options.
Nitroethanoic acid (nitroacetic acid) is weaker than trichloroethanoic acid.

questions.explanation: Trichloroacetic Acid (TCA) is a much stronger acid than all the acids mentioned in the options, from a chemical ionization standpoint, because the electronegative Chlorine atoms draw electron density away from the carboxyl end of the molecule, creating a partial positive charge on the carboxyl group, and allowing easier removal of the positively charged Hydrogen ion, H+.

questions.text: Benzyl acetate gives us which flavour?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Benzyl acetate does not typically have a raspberry flavor. Raspberry flavor is often associated with compounds such as raspberry ketone or raspberry esters.
Benzyl acetate does not have a distinct pineapple flavor. Pineapple flavor is usually associated with compounds such as ethyl butyrate or ethyl hexanoate.

Benzyl acetate (C6H5CH2OOCCH3) is an ester that is commonly used as a flavoring and fragrance agent. It has a jasmine-like fragrance and is used to impart a jasmine flavor in various products, such as perfumes, soaps, and food items.
Benzyl acetate is often used to enhance the flavor of apricot. It contributes to the fruity and sweet aroma of apricot flavor.

questions.explanation: Benzyl acetate (C6H5CH2OOCCH3) is an ester that is commonly used as a flavoring and fragrance agent. It has a jasmine-like fragrance and is used to impart a jasmine flavor in various products, such as perfumes, soaps, and food items.

questions.text: What happens if we react a carboxylic acid with an acyl chloride?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: When a carboxylic acid reacts with an acyl chloride, it forms an acid anhydride through the elimination of HCl. The general reaction can be represented as:RCOCl + R'COOH → RCOOCOR' + HCl

This option is not accurate. The reaction between a carboxylic acid and an acyl chloride does not typically result in the formation of an acyl halide. Acyl chlorides already contain a halogen atom (-Cl) in the acyl group, so there is no need for halogen substitution in this reaction.

options.text: It does not react due to resonance
options.explanation: It does not react due to resonance.This option is not accurate. Carboxylic acids can react with acyl chlorides, and the presence of resonance in carboxylic acids does not prevent the reaction.

options.text: It does not react due to bad leaving group
options.explanation: This option is not accurate. Acyl chlorides are highly reactive compounds and have good leaving groups (usually halogens such as chlorine or bromine). They can readily react with carboxylic acids, forming new compounds.

questions.explanation: When a carboxylic acid reacts with an acyl chloride, it forms an acid anhydride through the elimination of HCl. The general reaction can be represented as:RCOCl + R'COOH → RCOOCOR' + HCl

questions.text: On reacting with which of the following carboxylic acids produce CO2?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: CO2 gas is evolved when carboxylic acids react with carbonates and bicarbonates.
CO2 gas is evolved when carboxylic acids react with carbonates and bicarbonates.
When carboxylic acids react with bisulphites, SO2 gas is produced so it is incorrect option.

Carboxylic acids react with carbonates and bicarbonates to release CO2 gas.They react with Bisulphites to release SO2 gas.2RCOOH + Na2CO3(aq) → 2RCOO−Na+(aq) + H2O + CO2(g)RCOOH + NaHCO3(aq) → RCOO−Na+(aq) + H2O + CO2(g)

questions.explanation: Carboxylic acids react with carbonates and bicarbonates to release CO2 gas.They react with Bisulphites to release SO2 gas.2RCOOH + Na2CO3(aq) → 2RCOO−Na+(aq) + H2O + CO2(g)RCOOH + NaHCO3(aq) → RCOO−Na+(aq) + H2O + CO2(g)

questions.text: Which of the following acid solutions is used for the seasoning of food?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Butyric acid has a very unpleasant odor and cannot be used for seasoning food. Hence, the option is incorrect.
Phthalic acid is a dicarboxylic acid and is not used for seasoning. Hence, the option is incorrect.
Lactic acid is a weak acid that is produced as a result of anaerobic respiration, but it is also not used for seasoning food. Hence, the option is incorrect.
Acetic acid lowers the pH of food. It also gives the food an acidic taste and enhances its flavor. Hence, it is used for seasoning, and it is the correct option.

questions.explanation: Acetic acid lowers the pH of food, giving it an acidic taste. For example, salt and vinegar chips.

questions.text: Which type of carboxylic acid is produced from the hydrolysis of nitriles?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Beta-hydroxy carboxylic acids are a type of carboxylic acid that contains a hydroxyl group (-OH) attached to the beta carbon (the carbon next to the carboxyl group). These acids are formed when a carbonyl compound undergoes a reaction called aldol condensation, followed by dehydration. However, in the context of the hydrolysis of nitriles, beta-hydroxy carboxylic acids are not the products.

options.text: Acids, having one carbon more than the starting material
options.explanation: The type of carboxylic acid produced from the hydrolysis of nitriles is acids, having one carbon more than the starting material. This means that when a nitrile undergoes hydrolysis, it is converted into a carboxylic acid with one additional carbon atom compared to the original nitrile compound. So, the correct option is B) Acids, having one carbon more than the starting material.

options.text: Acids with one carbon less than the starting material
options.explanation: The number of carbon atoms in the main chain does not decrease; rather, it increases. Hence, this option is incorrect
Carboxylic acids already have a hydroxyl group on their alpha carbon as a part of their functional group. They cannot add more hydroxyl groups. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The type of carboxylic acid produced from the hydrolysis of nitriles is acids, having one carbon more than the starting material. This means that when a nitrile undergoes hydrolysis, it is converted into a carboxylic acid with one additional carbon atom compared to the original nitrile compound.

questions.text: Which of the following acid solution is used for the seasoning of food?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: There are different types of acid solutions used for seasoning of food, depending on the cuisine and dish being prepared.One common acid solution used for seasoning food is vinegar, which is a dilute solution of acetic acid. Vinegar is often used to add flavor and tanginess to salads, marinades, pickles, and sauces.Another acid solution used in some cuisines is lemon juice, which contains citric acid .

questions.text: Which of the following produce Carbon Dioxide with sodium bicarbonate?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: CH3COOCH3: This compound is dimethyl ether, and it does not react with sodium bicarbonate to produce carbon dioxide. It lacks a proton that can be donated to the bicarbonate ion.
CH3CH2OH: This compound is ethanol, and it does not react with sodium bicarbonate to produce carbon dioxide. It also lacks a proton to donate.

The substance that produces carbon dioxide with sodium bicarbonate is CH3COOH ( Acetic acid).When carboxylic acid reacts with sodium bicarbonate solution, carbon dioxide is evolved with a brisk effervescence along with sodium acetate and water.CH3COOH + NaHCO3 → CO2 +H2O + NaCH3COO
CH3COOOCH3: This compound is ethyl acetate, and it does not react with sodium bicarbonate to produce carbon dioxide. It does not contain a proton that can be donated to the bicarbonate ion.

questions.explanation: The substance that produces carbon dioxide with sodium bicarbonate is CH3COOH ( Acetic acid).When carboxylic acid reacts with sodium bicarbonate solution, carbon dioxide is evolved with a brisk effervescence along with sodium acetate and water.CH3COOH + NaHCO3 → CO2 +H2O + NaCH3COO

questions.text: Which of the following flavour is due to Amyl acetate ?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The Jasmine flavor is due to the Benzyl acetate.
The pineapple flavor is due to the ethyl butyrate.
The characteristic sweet and fruity scent of bananas is due to the presence of Amyl acetate.
The orange flavor is due to octyl acetate.

questions.explanation: Amyl acetate (A-mil AS-uh-tate) is a colorless liquid with a distinctive banana-like flavor and odor.

questions.text: Which of the following acid is used as a "coagulant" for latex in the rubber industry?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Acetic acid is used as a coagulant for latex in the rubber industry.Acetic acid on dissolution gives Hydrogen ions.Latex is the fluid used to make rubber and is found in trees.For coagulating: The hydrogen ions of Acetic acid have a positive charge which on reacting with latex which is negatively charged helps in the formation of coagulation.Hence, A is the correct option.
Butyric acid is used in the preparation of various butyrate esters. It is used to produce cellulose acetate butyrate (CAB), which is used in a wide variety of tools, paints, and coatings.
Propanoic acids are used in the pesticides and pharmaceutical industry. They are also used as preservatives

options.text: All of these options are correct
options.explanation: It is an incorrect option.

questions.explanation: OPTION A: Acetic acid is used as a coagulant for latex in the rubber industry.Acetic acid on dissolution gives Hydrogen ions.Latex is the fluid used to make rubber and is found in trees.For coagulating: The hydrogen ions of Acetic acid have a positive charge which on reacting with latex which is negatively charged helps in the formation of coagulation.Hence, A is the correct option.OPTION B: Butyric acid is used in the preparation of various butyrate esters. It is used to produce cellulose acetate butyrate (CAB), which is used in a wide variety of tools, paints, and coatings.OPTION C: Propanoic acids are used in the pesticides and pharmaceutical industry. They are also used as preservatives.OPTION D: It is an incorrect option.

questions.text: Acetic acid can be used in _ ?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Acetic acid is mainly used in the form of vinegar with the pungent smell. As it appears in the liquid state, it is used as a preservative in pickles.
In paints, Acrylic acid is used mainly. It particularly forms polymers. Its uses include plastics, coatings, adhesives, elastomers, paints, and polishes.

This option is incorrect. In pesticides: Acetic acid is not typically used as a pesticide. While it might have some minor effects on certain pests, it's not effective enough to be used as a primary pesticide.
All of these: Since not all of the individual options are correct, this choice is also incorrect.

questions.explanation: Acetic acid is mainly used in the form of vinegar with the pungent smell. As it appears in the liquid state, it is used as a preservative in pickles.

questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Acetic acid also known as ethanoic acid (CH3COOH) can form hydrogen bonds with water and alcohol since all three species have OH group. It is also soluble in ethers due to their polar nature.

HBr and HI are less polar due to smaller electronegativity difference between hydrogen and bromine/iodine resulting in weaker permanent dipole-permanent dipole interactions between acetic acid and HBr or HI hence acetic acid will not fully dissolve.
Some bromine reacts with water to form HBr and HOBr in bromine water. Br2 is a non polar molecule and there will only be weak instantaneous dipole induced dipole forces between Br2 and acetic acid.
Only option A is correct.
